purpose: Phase 4 Network Architecture Matrix for the Epstein Command Center. This layer explains the functions visible in the records: access, logistics, money, reputation repair, legal pressure, silence, media leverage, institutional proximity, and uncertainty control.
boundary: This matrix exposes network function, not automatic criminal guilt. A record can prove contact, money, travel, scheduling, testimony, contradiction, settlement, or institutional access. Criminal attribution requires conviction, plea, court finding, authenticated exhibit, admission, or clearly supported legal record.
speculationRule: Speculation is allowed only as a labelled question. It must state what record triggered the question, what is missing, and what would confirm or falsify the theory.

functions.title: Access Network
functions.evidenceClass: Documented association / contact / invitation / meeting trail
functions.whatToTrack: Who had access, who introduced whom, who kept contact after conviction, who appears across emails, schedules, court filings, visitor records, or public reporting.
redFlags: Private access without clear professional purpose
Cross-over between politics, finance, academia, media, royalty, science, and philanthropy
Contradictions between records and public statements

functions.title: Logistics Network
functions.whatToTrack: Who arranged travel, rooms, appointments, massage schedules, errands, accommodation, flights, and property access.
redFlags: Travel and accommodation around vulnerable persons
Records that connect names, dates, properties, and assistants
Denials of knowledge that require cross-checking

functions.title: Money / Legitimacy Network
functions.whatToTrack: Payments, charitable introductions, institutional donations, consulting claims, science or academic access, foundations, wealth managers, and reputation-building transactions.
redFlags: Institutions accepting access or money despite known risk

functions.title: Reputation Repair Network
functions.whatToTrack: How Epstein or connected figures sought prestige, intellectual cover, media framing, elite introductions, charitable association, and public rehabilitation.
redFlags: Academic or science access used for legitimacy

functions.title: Legal Pressure / Silence Network
functions.whatToTrack: Settlements, NDAs, threat letters, sealed records, delayed disclosures, legal denials, retractions, discovery fights, and subpoena boundaries.
redFlags: Witnesses needing subpoenas to speak freely

functions.title: Media / Political Leverage Network
functions.evidenceClass: Email / strategy / public denial / timing lane
functions.whatToTrack: Emails or reporting showing how Epstein-related information could become political leverage, reputation leverage, debate-stage risk, or media framing.
redFlags: Timing around elections or public scandals
Private emails discussing how a public figure should respond
Journalists or advisers acting as narrative brokers
Claims used as leverage without court finding

functions.title: Institutional Failure Network
functions.whatToTrack: Missed warnings, weak prosecutions, plea deals, institutional tolerance, poor victim protection, delayed disclosure, and failures by law enforcement, universities, charities, banks, or powerful institutions.
redFlags: Public trust damaged by redactions or secrecy

functions.title: Speculation Quarantine
functions.whatToTrack: Questions raised by record patterns that are not yet proved: intelligence-adjacent access, possible leverage operations, unexplained money flows, protected networks, or coordinated silence.

readerMethod: Start with the file or court record.
Classify the evidence before naming the implication.
Map the function: access, logistics, money, reputation, law, silence, media, institution, or open question.
Look for repeated appearances across independent records.
Show the boundary on every claim.
Turn open questions into research tasks, not fake conclusions.
